bids.reasoning: It is the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0) and the opponent has opened with an offer that yields my lowest possible utility on all issues. I will counter by asserting my ideal preferences to establish a strong starting position, proposing the maximum utility for myself across the board.

The negotiation has just started (t=0.02), and the opponent has repeated their opening offer which provides me with minimum utility (0.20). I will show a slight willingness to compromise by conceding marginally on my least important issue (issueD) while maintaining my most preferred values for all other issues, aiming for an initial high utility of 0.973.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer of all 'valueD'. Because it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will continue to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) while making a minor concession on my lowest-weight issue (issueD) to signal a willingness to move.

The opponent made their first small concession on issueA, but they are still demand valueC which drops my utility significantly on my most important issue. Since we are very early in the negotiation (t=0.06), I need to hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB). I will offer a moderate concession by shifting issueC down to valueB while keeping issueD at valueC, exploring whether they value issueC more than I do.

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.08). The opponent has shown a minor concession on issueA, which is my most important issue, but they remain anchored to valueD for all other issues. To encourage further movement on the high-weight issues (issueA and issueB), I will fully concede to their opening preference on my lowest-priority issue, issueD, while holding strong on the rest.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.10). The opponent has made a marginal concession on issueA, offering valueC, but is holding exceptionally firm on all other issues with valueD. I will concede slightly on issueB to 'valueB' to explore if they are willing to reciprocate, while holding firm on my most important issue (issueA) and conceding fully on my least important (issueD) to maintain a high level of utility for myself (above 0.83).

The opponent is gradually conceding on issueA (my most important issue), dropping from valueD to valueC and now valueB. Since my importance for issueA is the highest, I will continue to hold firm on valueA to see if they concede further. To reciprocate their movement, I will make a minor concession on my less important issues while maintaining a high overall utility (above 0.85) given we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.12).

The negotiation is still in the early stages (t=0.14), but the opponent has made small concessions on my most important issue (`issueA`), shifting from `valueD` to `valueC` and now `valueB`. Since `issueA` carries the highest weight for me, I should hold firm on `valueA` while making gradual concessions on less critical issues like `issueC` and `issueD` to explore whether the opponent values them more highly. I will bid `valueB` for both `issueB` and `issueC`, and `valueC` for `issueD`, keeping my overall utility above 0.8.

The opponent is showing flexibility by exploring concessions on issueB while reverting their previous concessions on issueA. Since I value issueA and issueB highly (combined importance of 0.7), I will prioritize maintaining top-tier values for these issues. I will offer a moderate concession on issueC and issueD to see if giving ground on my less important issues encourages the opponent to accept high values on my key issues.

The opponent recently shifted their concessions from issueA to issueB, proposing valueC for issueB instead of valueD. To test if they highly value my concessions on issueB, I will offer valueC for issueB while keeping my most important issues (issueA, issueC, and issueD) at my maximum utility valueA. Since it's still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), this targeted offer maintains a high overall utility for me (0.84) while possibly aligning with their newfound flexibility on issueB.

The opponent has made a significant concession on the most important issue (issueA: valueA). Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.20), I will lock in the opponent's concession on issueA and offer a moderate compromise on issues B, C, and D. By offering valueB for issues B and C, and valueC for issueD, I aim to maintain my utility above 0.8 while moving towards an acceptable middle ground for the issues my opponent seems to value most.

Given the time fraction is still early (t=0.22), I am targeting an offer with a utility around 0.81. The opponent has recently shown a willingness to concede on issueA (offering valueA at t=0.20) and issueC (offering valueC at t=0.22). I will match their latest concession on issueC and hold firm on issueA, while reclaiming utility on issueB and issueD to keep my overall return high.

The opponent continues to prefer 'valueD' across most issues, though they have recently begun exploring 'valueC' on issueA and issueB. I will target a utility of approximately 0.78 by holding firm on my most valuable issue (issueA: valueA) and moderately conceding on issueB and issueC. Since my weight on issueD is the lowest (0.1), I will grant the opponent's preferred 'valueD' to see if this elicits a reciprocal concession on the higher-weight issues.

The opponent's recent offers show a willingness to concede on my highly valued issues individually (e.g., offering `valueA` for `issueA` at t=0.20 and `valueB` for `issueB` at t=0.26). Since we are nearing the end of the early negotiation phase (t=0.26), I will hold firm on my most important issue (`issueA`) while making gradual concessions on my lower-weight issues (`issueC` and `issueD`) to find a workable trade-off that maintains a utility over 0.8.

The time fraction is 0.28, so we are still relatively early in the negotiation. The opponent is testing minor concessions on individual issues across different offers (such as bidding `valueB` on `issueB`, `valueA` on `issueA`, and `valueC` on `issueD`). Since they have demonstrated some flexibility on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), I will hold fast on `issueA` at `valueA` and compromise slightly on `issueB` and `issueC` to propose a new permutation targeting roughly ~0.84 utility for me.

The opponent is consistently holding firm on low-utility offers such as valueD for multiple issues, although they occasionally experiment with other values like valueC for issueC or valueB for issueB. At t=0.3, I am still in the early-mid game, so I should maintain a relatively high utility for myself. I will propose a combination that secures my highest-weighted issue (issueA) while offering concessions in the lower-weighted issues (such as issueC and issueD) that the opponent has indicated interest in.

I am maintaining my top preferences on my most heavily weighted issues (issueA and issueB, combined weight 0.7) to secure a high baseline utility. In exchange, I am offering a full concession on issueC to 'valueD'—which the opponent has requested continuously—and moving issueD to 'valueC', aligning with a value they previously floated at t=0.28. This presents a strong compromise proposal that achieves a utility of ~0.79 for me.

The negotiation is in the early-mid phase (t=0.34). The opponent has continued to explore compromises on individual issues (showing flexibility on issueB and issueC recently), although they generally stick to valueD. To remain constructive while protecting my key interests, I will hold my most heavily weighted issue (issueA) at its maximum value and propose acceptable alternatives across the remaining issues. This offer gives an overall utility in the upper 0.7 quartile, creating a diverse tradeoff package.

The opponent has shown consistent interest in `valueC` and `valueD` for `issueC` and `issueD`. Since my importance weights for `issueA` and `issueB` are much higher, I will maintain top values (`valueA`) for those issues to secure high utility (0.814) while offering the opponent their seemingly preferred values on the lower-weight issues. Given the relatively early time fraction (t=0.36), I am not pressured to make large concessions yet.

The current time is early-mid (t=0.38). The opponent continues to rotate small concessions across different issues, most recently yielding on issueC but remaining steadfast on the others. To encourage a transition to mutual value discovery, I will maintain my top priority (issueA) at its maximum value and keep my second highest (issueB) at a reasonably high utility, while rotating concessions on lower-weight issues (issueC and issueD).

At time 0.40, the opponent is consistently requesting `valueD` or `valueC` for most issues, particularly on `issueC` and `issueD`. My highest importance issues are `issueA` and `issueB`, where I need `valueA` to maintain strong utility. To find common ground while protecting a solid utility of 0.76 for myself, I will fully concede `issueC` and `issueD` to `valueD`, while demanding my preferred `valueA` on my most critical issues.

The current time is t=0.42, and the opponent just requested `issueC: valueB` and `issueB: valueD`. I will maintain my top preference on my most heavily weighted issue (`issueA`) while extending concessions on the lower-weighted issues. I will propose a combination that includes `issueC: valueB` (requested by the opponent at t=0.38 and t=0.42) and `issueB: valueB` (requested at t=0.32), aiming for a utility of around 0.86 to keep early-game leverage while matching some of the opponent's recent demands.

At time t=0.44, we are approaching the mid-game and the opponent's offers are exploring various values across all issues. I will target an offer with a robust utility for myself (~0.89) while proposing concessions on issueB and issueD, which are slightly lower in weight for me but might help signal flexibility. I will hold firm on issueA, which is my highest-utility issue.

Approaching the mid-point of the negotiation (t=0.46), I am holding firm on my most valuable issue (issueA) and maintaining relatively high utility for issueB (valueB). To make the offer more attractive to the opponent, I will concede heavily on issues C and D (valueC and valueD), responding to their strong preference for lower-tier values on those issues. This balance yields a utility around 0.73, making progress while protecting my vital interests.

The opponent's most recent offer surprisingly included my top preference for issueB (valueA), and previously they have proposed valueD for issueC and valueB for issueD. To show cooperation near the midpoint (t=0.48), I will incorporate all of these opponent-proposed values (B: valueA, C: valueD, D: valueB) into my next bid. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue A to maintain a satisfactory utility near 0.81.

The opponent's most recent offer includes `valueC` for both `issueC` and `issueD`. Since these issues have the lowest importance to me (weights 0.2 and 0.1 respectively), I will concede and incorporate both of those values to signal cooperation. I will hold firm on `issueA` and `issueB` (my highest priorities) to maintain a strong overall utility (~0.84) at the halfway point of the negotiation.

The opponent's latest offer yields a utility of ~0.626, predominantly because they finally offered my top-priority issue (issueA) at its maximum value for me, while also proposing valueB for issueC, which is a decent compromise. Since we are halfway through the timeline, I will accept their terms for issues A, C, and D, but counter with 'valueB' for issueB to maintain a strong utility of 0.785 for myself. This anchors us close to an agreement while conceding on issues with lower weights.

I am at time fraction t=0.54, entering the middle portion of the negotiation. Analyzing the opponent's recent behaviors, they seem increasingly flexible, notably exploring combinations like `issueB: valueC` and `issueC: valueC` at t=0.54, and previously entertaining `issueA: valueA`. In this offer, I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB), while compromising on issueC to meet them where they showed interest recently, bringing a new value combination to the table. This keeps my utility very high (~0.86) while maintaining movement.

At t=0.56, we are past the midpoint of the negotiation, so I need to begin making reasonable tradeoffs. Since issueA and issueB are my highest-weight issues, I will hold firm on valueA for both, preserving the bulk of my utility. Observing the opponent's bidding history, they strongly resist conceding on issueD, frequently offering valueD, and have recently proposed valueB for issueC, so I will concede on these lower-weight issues (issueC to valueB and issueD to valueD) to encourage an agreement without significantly hurting my overall utility.

The negotiation has passed the halfway point (t=0.58). The opponent's last offer showed a uniform preference for valueC across all issues, but earlier offers (e.g., t=0.48 and t=0.52) indicated they are willing to accept valueA or valueB for issues A and B if they get favorable terms on issues C or D. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (A and B) but offer valueB for issueB to test if a slight concession brings us closer, and give valueC for issueC and valueC for issueD to directly match their recent signals on those lower-weight issues.

The opponent's most recent offer includes `issueA: valueA` and `issueD: valueB`, which provides a foundation I can build on since issueA is my most heavily weighted priority. However, their proposed values for issueB and issueC reduce my utility significantly. With time at t=0.60, I am consolidating on `valueA` for issueA and aligning with their proposal of `valueB` for issueD, while countering with moderate-to-high values for me on issues B and C to maintain an acceptable overall utility around 0.78.

The current time fraction is 0.62, so we are transitioning into the late-middle stage of the negotiation where identifying compromises is essential. The opponent's offer at t=0.60 showed a willingness to concede on my most critical issue (`issueA: valueA`) in exchange for concessions on other issues (`issueC: valueD`, `issueD: valueB`). I will construct a bid that matches three of the four values from their t=0.60 offer while holding slightly firmer on my second-most important issue (`issueB: valueB`) to maintain a favorable overall utility of ~0.73.

The opponent's latest offer provides a moderate amount of utility (around 0.67), showing willingness to compromise on issues A, B, and C by reaching out to 'valueB'. Since we are at t=0.64, I'm ready to move toward a more significant concession while maintaining a target utility of ~0.78 by offering an alternative mix. I will match their latest offer's values on A and C, bump issue B to 'valueA' (which they have offered in the past), and ask for a slight concession on issue D.

The opponent's latest offer gives me my highest-priority value for issueA, which brings the utility up to 0.682. Since we are at t=0.66, I can lock in their concession on issueA while compromising moderately on the remaining issues. I will propose a bid with valueA for issueA and valueB/valueC for the others to maintain my utility around 0.81, targeting values they have recently shown some flexibility toward.

The current time fraction is 0.68, so I am beginning to look for combinations of values the opponent has recently proposed scattered across their offers. The opponent recently offered 'valueB' for issueB at t=0.68 and t=0.64, 'valueB' for issueC at t=0.64, and 'valueB' for issueD at t=0.68. I will propose 'valueA' for issueA to maintain high utility in my most heavily weighted issue, along with 'valueB' for the remaining issues to capitalize on their recent concessions.

The time fraction is 0.7, so the deadline is approaching but we still have room to negotiate. I will target a utility of ~0.84 by holding firm on my most important issue (issueA: valueA), while adopting the opponent's recent concessions on other issues. Specifically, I am directly matching the values they just offered for issueB, issueC, and issueD in their most recent bid (t=0.70) to find a mutually acceptable compromise.

The opponent's most recent offer gave me my top value for issueB (valueA), but demanded poor values for issues A, C, and D. Since time is at 0.72 and we are entering the late stage, I need to start locking in acceptable concessions while prioritizing my highest-weight issue (issueA). I will accept the opponent's preferred values for issues C and D from their latest offer, taking the concession on issueB, but assert my preference for issueA (valueA) to maintain a strong overall utility (~0.81).

The time fraction is 0.74, indicating we are entering the late stages of negotiation. The opponent's recent offers prominently feature `issueB: valueA` and configurations involving valueC or valueD on other issues. To reach a viable compromise while maintaining solid utility, I will concede slightly on `issueA` to `valueB` (which the opponent has periodically accepted or proposed, such as at t=0.48 and t=0.64), provide their preferred `valueA` for `issueB`, and aim for mid-tier values on the less important issues.

The opponent's most recent offer gave me my most preferred value for my highest-weight issue (`issueA: valueA`) along with acceptable values for the minor issues, yielding a solid utility of 0.734. Given that we still have time until the deadline, I will accept their proposed values for `issueA`, `issueC`, and `issueD`, but attempt a compromise on `issueB` (proposing `valueB` instead of their `valueC`) to see if we can reach an agreement near ~0.8 utility before I concede further.

The time fraction is 0.78, so we are entering the final quarter of the negotiation. The opponent recently offered 'valueA' on issueB (at t=0.70, 0.72, 0.74) and just offered 'valueB' on issueA, issueC, and issueD at t=0.78. By combining their favored values for issues A, C, and D from their most recent offer with 'valueA' for issueB, I can present a highly targeted compromise that still provides me with a strong utility of ~0.81.

The opponent has just made an offer that exactly matches one of my previous bids, yielding my top preferred values for the two most important issues (issueA and issueB). This offer grants me a substantial utility of 0.841. With the deadline approaching at t=0.80, I will replicate this exact bid to formalize our agreement and ensure a successful negotiation.
